overview: This Vintage combo deck leverages a suite of tutors to assemble Oath of Druids, which draws cards and tutors for a win condition. The primary goal is to cast Show and Tell to bypass mana costs and bring in Atraxa, Grand Unifier. Atraxa's death trigger then generates infinite card advantage and mana to close out the game.
win_conditions: Casting Oath of Druids to draw into and tutor for Atraxa, Grand Unifier
Using Show and Tell to cast Atraxa or other high-cost threats for free
Leveraging Atraxa's death trigger to generate infinite resources and overwhelm the opponent

keep_criteria: Hands with multiple tutors to find Oath of Druids
Hands containing Show and Tell with early mana acceleration
Hands with early interaction to protect combo pieces
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetch/shock potential), one of Oath of Druids or Show and Tell, and at least one disruption or mana source.
Keep hands with Atraxa/Emrakul + 2+ mana sources.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking tutors or mana sources
Hands with only high-cost cards and no draw
Hands vulnerable to early disruption without answers
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with multiple Oath/Show and Tell but no mana, or hands with only disruption and no way to cast it.

early_game: Play mana rocks and tutors to locate Oath of Druids
Use early interaction to counter opponent's disruption
Prioritize drawing cards to find combo pieces
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize/Force of Will/Spell Pierce, or play a shock land.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast disruption, or begin assembling mana for Oath/Show and Tell.
Prioritize fetching dual lands and maintaining open mana for counterspells until you commit to your combo.
mid_game: Cast Oath of Druids and activate its ability to draw and tutor
Search for Atraxa, Grand Unifier or Show and Tell
Begin generating card advantage and setting up the win
Conserve mana for counterspells and disruption until you can safely resolve Oath or Show and Tell.
Use Lilian, the Last Hope to refill your hand and generate value.
Avoid overcommitting to Oath if you lack protection or a follow-up threat.
Commit threats like Atraxa or Emrakul when you have protection (counterspells, discard, or board wipe) or when the opponent has no removal.
Hold back Oath of Druids if the opponent has sweepers or targeted hate, as it requires tapping your creatures.
late_game: Use Atraxa's death trigger to draw cards and tutor for more threats
Cast Show and Tell to bypass mana costs for game-ending cards
Overwhelm opponent with infinite resources and unblockable threats
Atraxa, Grand Unifier, or Emrakul, World Aflame resolved via Oath of Druids or Show and Tell.
Atraxa wins through +1/+1 counters and token generation
Emrakul wins by drawing cards and removing threats
Grand Unifier wins by answering multiple problems and enabling attacks.

Go all-in when you have protection for your finisher, the opponent has no sweepers or targeted hate in hand, and you can follow up with additional value (Lilian, Emrakul’s draw step, or Atraxa’s tokens).
Commit to Oath/Show and Tell when you can safely resolve it and have a clear path to victory.

key_cards.name: Oath of Druids
key_cards.notes: Cast it, then activate its ability to draw cards and tutor for a creature or artifact. Prioritize finding Atraxa or Show and Tell.

key_cards.name: Atraxa, Grand Unifier
key_cards.notes: When it dies, draw cards and tutor for a creature. Use it to generate infinite value or find Show and Tell.

key_cards.name: Show and Tell
key_cards.notes: Cast it to put a creature onto the battlefield without paying its mana cost. Use it to cast Atraxa or other high-cost threats immediately.

sequencing_tips: Always tutor for Atraxa first when possible to enable Show and Tell or further tutoring
Hold up interaction for opponent's known disruption before committing to Oath of Druids
Use Show and Tell to cast Atraxa if you lack the mana, then use Atraxa's death trigger to find more pieces
Oath of Druids and Show and Tell are sorceries, making them vulnerable to counterspells
Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Emrakul are your primary win conditions, so protect them at all costs.
Lilian, the Last Hope is a critical engine for card advantage and mana fixing.
Avoid tapping your creatures for Oath if you have no follow-up or protection.
Watch for Leyline of the Void in the sideboard, as it can completely shut down your game plan.

matchups.advice: Prioritize finding Oath of Druids quickly and use Show and Tell to bypass counterspells. Keep interaction in hand to protect your combo pieces.
Use early interaction to slow down aggressive strategies. Rush to Oath of Druids and use Atraxa's death trigger to recover from board wipes or aggressive pressure.
Race against other combo decks by optimizing tutor lines. Use Show and Tell to accelerate your win condition and keep interaction for their key pieces.

Use Force of Will, Spell Pierce, or split your combo pieces to resolve threats. Oath and Show and Tell are sorceries, so resolve them when the opponent has no counterspells, or use Atraxa/Emrakul which are harder to counter. Consider playing around counters with discard or timing.

Out-value them with Atraxa/Emrakul and Lilian. Use Thoughtseize to hit key ramp pieces (Mana Crypt, Arcane Signet, etc.). Atraxa’s +1/+1 counters and token generation will outscale their mana advantage.

Win by tracking key cards, sideboarding in Rest in Peace/Leyline, and out-sequence your opponent. Use Thoughtseize to hit their Oath/Show and Tell or protection. Resolve Atraxa/Emrakul first, as it often wins the race. Be cautious of their Lilian and counterspells.

common_threats: Counterspells targeting Oath of Druids or Show and Tell
Board wipes that clear Atraxa before it can trigger
Watch for Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void, and graveyard hate.
Track opponent’s hand for sweepers (Wrath of God, Anger of the Gods) and targeted removal (Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares).
Note when they run out of counterspells to safely resolve your finishers.
